name: parallax
description: Parallax — independent paths, one verified result. A spec-driven, parallel, blind-coder TDD pipeline: a maximally concrete read-only spec drives two independent tracks (a test-writer and a blind-coder that never sees the tests); a single whole-seeing arbiter runs the tests and loops with failure analysis until green, then pushes a feature branch. Optionally adds an independent cross-model verifier — a provider chain of non-Claude models (Codex primary, optional fallbacks like Gemini, CLI or API) — that adversarially reviews the spec before the blind tracks and each green slice after (opt-in via .parallax/codex.toml). An autonomous driver (/parallax:auto, --from-doc) runs the whole cycle from a written brief with no human gates — independent slices build in parallel waves, the cross-model verifier replaces the human gate, and anything genuinely ambiguous is parked for review; headless and schedulable, and nothing reaches main without a human. On a usage limit the verifier falls back to the next provider, and if all are limited the run checkpoints and resumes from the same place on an hourly retry, losing no progress. Optional Telegram notifications report the autonomous run's progress (verbose) or just its lifecycle and limit/resume events. Runs in Claude Code on subscription limits.
